land under the lumber act, knowing
that it contained valuable deposits
of coal.


Three of Walkers assistants
were in the mine when he was kill
ed. They claim that their ladders
were cut and that they had a diff
cult time in reaching the surface,
only to find their chief cold in
death.


The weapon used was sawed-off
shotgun, a common weapon in Du-
range, especially among the land
owners-a class who armed them
selves with these weapons to fight
the Western Federation of Miners
during thee famous Colorado war.


Walker was known as a shrewd
man and was an important witness
for the government at the hearings
before the grand pury. Many men
who have accumulated fortunes
through alleged frauds wept no
tears at his untimely death.


Immediately following the trag-
edy orders were sent for all secret
service men in the district to con-
gregate at Denver. The meeting
was secret and orders were re-
ceived from Chief Wilkie to ferret
the assassination to the bottom.
